name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #4
description: Florentine Groteschi #4 is a blue screen reliquary single-panel relic built around Botticelli and Cursed Auction House UI. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Speranza, used here as the witness card. Cosimo's private library shelfmark gets pulled into a CAPTCHA grid asking which saints are traffic lights, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The generated insert, a cherub holding a QR code like a cursed coupon, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks GYATT at Σ 438.

attributes.value: a cherub holding a QR code like a cursed coupon

chess_piece.name: Bishop

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.history_now_bridge: MEDICI RECEIPT DRAMA: Cosimo's private library shelfmark gets pulled into a CAPTCHA grid asking which saints are traffic lights. Speranza reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a cherub holding a QR code like a cursed coupon

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Botticelli anchors the historical source. Speranza supplies the witness logic. The cherub holding a QR code like a cursed coupon acts as the modern meme-object. The blue screen reliquary palette and Cursed Auction House UI styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Botticelli is treated as evidence in a medici receipt drama. The old-world clue is Cosimo's private library shelfmark; the modern-world echo is a CAPTCHA grid asking which saints are traffic lights. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Speranza card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. Cosimo approves the lighting and ignores the crime. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post
charisma or persuasive charm; patronage influence rephrased as social magic

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: Do not let the codon marker breed near the provenance strip.
Do not cite the notary after midnight.
This file has already been read by one Medici accountant.
If the Tarocchi card and QR code disagree, trust the card.
If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.
